title: Xenia and Bit Operations
description: You have an array of 2^n integers. Build a segment tree where odd levels compute OR and even levels compute XOR. Process q point updates and after each update print the root value.
input_spec: The first line contains two integers n and q (1 <= n <= 17, 1 <= q <= 10^5). The second line contains 2^n integers. Each of the next q lines contains two integers p and b, meaning set a[p] = b.
output_spec: After each update, print the root value of the segment tree.

routes.name: Segment tree with alternating operations
routes.description: Build a segment tree where the merge operation alternates between OR and XOR based on the level.

observations.title: Level determines the operation
observations.description: At the leaf level (level 0), values are stored directly. Level 1 merges pairs with OR, level 2 with XOR, and so on alternating.

hints.nudge: The problem says odd levels use OR and even levels use XOR. How does this map to your tree structure?
hints.question: If leaves are at level 0, what operation combines two leaves? What combines two of those results?
hints.formal: At each internal node, check its depth from the leaves. If depth is odd: OR children. If depth is even: XOR children. (Or vice versa depending on 0-indexing.)

observations.title: Standard segment tree structure
observations.description: Use a 1-indexed array of size 4 * 2^n. Build bottom-up, update by changing leaf then propagating up to root.

hints.nudge: How is a segment tree typically stored in an array?
hints.question: If a node is at index i, where are its children? How do you propagate a leaf change to the root?
hints.formal: tree[1] = root. Children of tree[i] are tree[2i] and tree[2i+1]. Update: set leaf, then walk up parent = i/2, recomputing merge(left, right).

observations.title: Propagate with correct operation per level
observations.description: When propagating up after an update, each node must use the correct operation (OR or XOR) for its level.

hints.nudge: When you propagate up, does every node use the same merge operation?
hints.question: After updating a leaf, you walk to the root updating each node. How does each node know which operation to use?
hints.formal: Store the depth of each node (or compute from position). When recomputing node i: if depth is odd, tree[i] = tree[2i] | tree[2i+1]; if even, tree[i] = tree[2i] ^ tree[2i+1].

observations.title: O(n) per update, O(q * n) total
observations.description: Each update modifies one leaf and propagates up O(n) levels to the root. With q updates, total is O(q * n).

hints.nudge: How many nodes change when you update one leaf?
hints.question: A leaf update propagates to the root. How many nodes are on that path?
hints.formal: Path length from leaf to root is n (since array has 2^n elements). Total complexity: O(2^n) build + O(q * n) updates. With n <= 17, this is fast enough.
